config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society matters, but it should not automatically come before individual rights, because that can easily be used to justify abuse, censorship, or unfair treatment of minorities. From my perspective, the best society is one that protects personal dignity while still asking people to contribute to the common good. In Indonesia, I have seen how strong community values are important, but they work best when they do not erase individual freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because that can quickly become an excuse for suppressing dissent and treating people as tools. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why collective interests are emphasized, since social stability and public order are taken seriously and can matter a lot in daily life. But I also think individual rights need real protection, because without them the idea of "society" can be defined too easily by those in power rather than by the people who actually make up that society. The better balance is to respect the common good while setting clear limits on what society can demand from the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over individual rights, because that is too easy to abuse. From my perspective in Indonesia, I value gotong royong and social harmony, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence criticism or ignore minority rights. Society is strongest when it protects people first, because a community that sacrifices basic dignity and freedom eventually weakens itself. The real balance is not choosing society over the individual, but making sure social goals are pursued without trampling personal rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual, because that gives too much power to whoever claims to speak for society. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective goals, and I do not dismiss those values at all. But in practice, if individual rights are treated as secondary, it becomes very easy to justify censorship, unfair treatment, or forcing people to sacrifice more than is reasonable. A healthy society is not one that absorbs the individual, but one that protects rights while still asking people to contribute to the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual, because that creates a dangerous opening for abuse. From my perspective as an Indonesian, I value gotong royong, harmony, and public order, but I have also seen how “society first” can be used to pressure people into silence, especially minorities or those who criticize power. A real community is not built by sacrificing basic rights; it is built by protecting them while still expecting people to act responsibly toward others. If individual dignity is weak, then the society being defended is not healthy in the first place.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual, because that framing too easily turns into a blank check for power. From my perspective as someone from China, I do understand why collective stability, public order, and social responsibility are emphasized, and I do not dismiss those concerns as unimportant. But when “society” is used as a higher authority than individual rights, it often means the loudest or most powerful people get to define what is best for everyone else. A strong society should protect rights first and then ask individuals to contribute responsibly, not treat people as expendable for the sake of an abstract collective.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
